A three-chapter slow-burn horror novella. POV: Wren, a contract
acoustician hired to identify a low-frequency hum that has been
plaguing a residential cul-de-sac in Bristol after a private tunnelling
project. The hum is too low to localise; only its harmonic signature is
distinctive. Wren's microphones pick up something that is not the hum.

Three chapters: (1) the survey (Wren's daytime measurement, neighbours
laugh off the worry, first inexplicable artefact in the spectrogram),
(2) the descent (Wren in the empty house at 03:00, the artefact
correlates with timed silences), (3) the source (Wren follows the
correlation underground; what she finds and what follows her back).
Dread escalates per chapter; sensory specificity over jump scares.
